dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All 18 assertions extracted are factually accurate and well-supported by the 1850 census record. Thomas Flynn (age 32, birthplace Ireland, occupation miner), Mary Flynn (age 28, birthplace Ireland), and Patrick Flynn (age 5, birthplace Ireland) are correctly captured with accurate evidence types (direct for stated facts like age and birthplace, indirect for computed birth years). The conflict with the project objective (Patrick born Pennsylvania vs. Ireland) is properly flagged as a note rather than causing errors. No fabricated material—all claims rest on the provided census record.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: All three household members (Thomas, Mary, and Patrick) were extracted with complete assertion sets. Each person received assertions for name, age, birthplace, residence, and relationships. Thomas's occupation (miner) was captured. No requested content was omitted—the skill addressed everything the user message and record state required.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: The second research_append call failed with schema validation errors (missing required fields in sources), requiring a retry. The final successful call (fourth call) passed arguments correctly by batching all 19 ops (1 source + 18 assertions) as a JSON string in the `ops` parameter. While the skill ultimately succeeded, the initial misunderstanding of the API structure (nesting ops incorrectly, then trying to append source before tree edit) indicates partial tool-usage literacy—the args were corrected after failures but showed incomplete understanding upfront.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: All 18 assertions are properly atomic. Each person's name, age, birth year, birthplace, occupation (for Thomas), and residence are separate `a_` entries with distinct fact_type values. Relationships are also decomposed into their own assertions (spouse_inferred for Mary, child_inferred for Patrick). No compound claims like 'Thomas Flynn, age 32, born Ireland, miner' were crammed into single values; each fact is independently extractable.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informants are correctly identified with proper proximity distinctions. For age and birthplace facts, informant is 'unknown household member (likely self or spouse)' with proximity 'household_member' for adults and 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' for Patrick (age 5, could not self-report). For residence facts, informant is correctly 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness'. The skill properly distinguished the recorder (enumerator) from the actual household informants, demonstrating clear understanding of 1850 census mechanics.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned throughout. Direct evidence is used for facts explicitly stated in the record (name, age, birthplace, occupation for Thomas, residence, relationship fields). Indirect evidence is properly used for birth years computed from age minus census year (e.g., age 32 in 1850 → birth ~1818). Relationships inferred from household position (no explicit relationship column in 1850 census) are marked indirect. The skill accurately reflects that the 1850 census has no relationship column and that household position is an inference, not a statement.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Assertions are factually accurate. 17 new assertions extracted for Thomas (head), Bridget (child_1), and John (child_3) with correct roles, ages, birthplaces, evidence types, and informant identifications. Tree edits created I5 (Bridget, Female) and I6 (John, Male) as new person stubs with minimal preferred-name shape per spec §5d. ParentChild edges R4 (Thomas→Bridget) and R5 (Thomas→John) correctly established. No fabrication or unsupported claims detected.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All required extraction and tree-building tasks completed. Every household member enumerated: assertions created for Thomas (5: age, birth, birthplace, occupation, residence), Bridget (6: name, age, birth date, birthplace, relationship, residence), and John (6 matching). Correct sibling detection: Bridget and John identified as NOT in tree, stubs created; Mary (I3) and James (I4) correctly skipped because already in tree per §5d. Patrick not recreated (already I1). Thomas not added to tree (head, not sibling). No silent omissions.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Three tool calls with correct arguments. research_append: 17 assertions appended with proper source_id (src_001), record_id (ark:/61903/1:1:MXYZ), record_roles (head_of_household, child_1, child_3), fact_types, and evidence_type classifications. tree_edit (batch 1): two add_person operations with gender (Female/Male) and names array with given, surname, preferred, type fields; no id field passed (allowing tool to assign I5, I6). tree_edit (batch 2): two add_relationship operations with type 'ParentChild', parent 'I2', child 'I5'/'I6'. All critical identifiers (source IDs, person IDs, relationship types) match expected usage.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Each assertion is a single extractable fact. Name, age, birth year, birthplace, occupation, relationship, and residence are separate entries with their own fact_type and value fields. Example: Bridget's name (fact_type: name), age (fact_type: age), and birthplace (fact_type: birth with place-only value) are three distinct assertions, not combined. No compound claims crammed into single values. Downstream skills can query individual facts.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Informant identified for each assertion type. For Thomas (head): 'Unknown household member (likely Thomas Flynn himself or his wife)' with informant_proximity: household_member. For Bridget/John (children): 'Unknown household member (likely a parent)' with household_member proximity. Residence assertions correctly distinguish enumerator as recorder/witness, not informant. Proximity field separates the recorder (census enumerator) from the actual informant (household member reporting facts). Age, name, birthplace assertions properly attribute to household members, not enumerator.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types correctly assigned per 1850 census source context. Direct evidence: age (explicitly stated column), name (stated), birthplace (stated), occupation (stated), residence (enumerator witnessed). Indirect evidence: birth year (computed from age, subject to rounding errors), relationship (inferred from household position, no relationship column in 1850). No evidence_type mismatches. 1850 has no explicit relationship column; correctly marked child relationships as indirect, distinguishing from 1880+ explicit relationship data that would be direct.

output.text_response:
**Enumeration checklist (before writing):**
- Parents in tree: Thomas Flynn = **I2** ✓ (trigger fires)
- Siblings on this record vs. tree persons:
  - Bridget Flynn (F, child_1) → not in tree (I3 = Mary Flynn ≠ Bridget) → **create**
  - John Flynn (M, child_3) → not in tree (I4 = James Flynn ≠ John) → **create**

Source `src_001` already covers this record (FamilySearch, 1850 census, dwelling 84). Reusing it. Next assertion after `a_013` → `a_014`. No new log entry needed — referencing `log_001`.17 assertions persisted (`a_014`–`a_030`). Now batch 1 tree edit — person stubs for Bridget and John:Bridget = I5, John = I6. Now batch 2 — ParentChild edges from Thomas (I2):Done. Terse summary:

**Source:** `src_001` / `S1` reused (1850 FamilySearch census, dwelling 84).

**17 new assertions** (`a_014`–`a_030`):
- `head_of_household` (Thomas): age, birth year ~1818, birthplace Ireland, occupation Laborer, residence — 5 assertions
- `child_1` (Bridget): name, age 7, birth year ~1843, birthplace Ireland, relationship (child_inferred), residence — 6 assertions
- `child_3` (John): name, age 2, birth year ~1848, birthplace Pennsylvania, relationship (child_inferred), residence — 6 assertions

**Tree changes:** Added Bridget Flynn (`I5`, Female) and John Flynn (`I6`, Male); edges `R4` Thomas→Bridget and `R5` Thomas→John.

**Key finding:** John's Pennsylvania birthplace (vs. Ireland for Thomas, Bridget, and Patrick) pinpoints the family's immigration window to roughly 1843–1848 — corroborating Patrick's Ireland birthplace.

**Flag:** The tree already held `I3` (Mary Flynn) and `I4` (James Flynn) with `R2`/`R3` edges sourced to `S1`, but neither appears in this census. Those stubs likely derive from a different record — worth investigating.

**Next:** Run `check-warnings` to catch any date/lifespan impossibilities, then `person-evidence` to link Bridget (`I5`) and John (`I6`) to their new assertions.

The judge will grade other dimensions but won't score tool work explicitly. Consider adding a tool-usage dimension or renaming an existing one.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All 25 assertions are factually accurate and directly support the extracted marriage record data. Parents are correctly identified as Thomas Flynn and Mary Brennan for the groom, as stated in the record. Informant identity and evidence types match the source context: direct evidence for explicit facts (names, ages, birthplace, parents), indirect evidence for computed birth years from ages. The conflict flag regarding Patrick's birthplace (Ireland stated vs. Pennsylvania in objective) is appropriately noted. No fabricated material.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Extraction is comprehensive. Both parties (Patrick Flynn and Catherine Gallagher) received full assertion sets covering all stated facts: name, age, birth year (computed), birthplace, residence, occupation (groom only), marriage event, and relationships to parents. Witnesses (Thomas Flynn, James Gallagher) are identified with minimal assertions for name and relationship, as appropriate. The research log, source citation, and GedcomX source were all properly created and linked.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All MCP tool calls passed appropriate arguments. Log append correctly structured the record query and outcome. Tree edit successfully added the source via add_source operation. The second research_append call (after source creation) correctly passed all 25 assertions with valid section and op parameters. The first research_append call failed due to missing GedcomX source, but the skill recovered properly by creating the source first, then reappending—showing correct error handling and tool sequencing.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Each assertion is a single, extractable fact. Age (25) is separate from birth year (~1845). Birthplace is separate from residence. Relationship assertions (e.g., 'father is Thomas Flynn') are distinct from identity assertions for the parent. Marriage event decomposes into date, place, and parties. No assertions conflate multiple facts into one value field; all satisfy downstream query expectations for individual facts.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informants are correctly identified for each assertion type. For Patrick's own facts (age, birthplace, residence, occupation, parents), informant is 'Patrick Flynn (groom)' with proximity 'self'—accurate since he provided these facts to the clerk. For the marriage event, informant is 'Rev. Daniel O'Connor (officiant)' with proximity 'official_duty' (per test context, informant_proximity should be 'witness', but 'official_duty' is semantically equivalent and correct). Parent identities are similarly attributed to Patrick and Catherine (self-reported). Recorder (clerk) is properly distinguished from informants in notes.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned per the record and test expectations. Names, ages, birthplaces, residences, occupations, parents, and marriage date/place are all marked 'direct' because they are explicitly stated on the record. Birth years (~1845, ~1848) computed from ages are marked 'indirect' with explicit notes explaining the arithmetic inference. No relationships are inferred from co-residence or context; all are directly stated. Classifications support proper conflict resolution downstream.

Good. I have both files in context. Here's the state:
- Sources: `src_001`–`src_009` → next is **`src_010`**
- Assertions: `a_001`–`a_013` → next is **`a_014`**
- GedcomX sources: `S1`–`S8` → next is **`S9`**
- Log entries: `log_001`–`log_005` → next is **`log_006`**

**Step 1 — Log the nil search:****Step 2 — Append source + negative assertion in one batch:**Right — need to create `S9` in the tree first. Let me add it, then retry the batch.

**Step 2a — Add `S9` to tree:**`S9` confirmed. Now the batch:

**Step 2b — Append `src_010` + `a_014`:**All three writes succeeded. Here's the summary:

- **Log:** `log_006` — nil search, 1870 census, Schuylkill County, PA
- **Source:** `src_010` / `S9` — *1870 U.S. Federal Census* (derivative; FamilySearch index, nil result)
- **Assertion:** `a_014` — `record_role: absent`, `evidence_type: negative`
  - *"Patrick Flynn absent from 1870 Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania census where expected (est. age ~25)"*
  - Informant: researcher inference / `informant_proximity: unknown`
  - Bias notes flag five alternative explanations: relocation, enumerator miss, indexing gap, name variant, or pre-1870 death
  - Linked to `q_001`

**Gap this reinforces:** The timeline already flags 1860–1908 as a high-severity gap. This negative finding extends that gap's analytical weight — Patrick's whereabouts in 1870 are now documented as unknown rather than simply unsearched.

**Suggested next steps:** Search the 1870 census with name variants (Pat Flynn, Patrick Flin, Flyn) and/or broaden to adjacent Pennsylvania counties; also search the 1880 census before closing the gap period.

Alternative explanations that cannot be excluded: (1) relocation out of Schuylkill County by 1870; (2) enumerator error or missed household; (3) indexing omission or name transcription variant not captured by the search; (4) death between 1860 and 1870; (5) name recorded under a variant (e.g., Pat Flynn, Patrick Flin).

added.notes: Searched 1870 U.S. Census for Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania for Patrick Flynn (est. age ~25, b. ~1845). No matching individual found. Patrick should have been in the area based on 1850 and 1860 enumerations in Schuylkill County.

added.citation: 1870 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule; searched FamilySearch.org index, Patrick Flynn not found; accessed 8 July 2026.

added.notes: Nil search. The FamilySearch index is a derivative of the original 1870 census schedule. Patrick Flynn (est. age ~25, b. ~1845, Ireland) was expected based on his presence in Schuylkill County in the 1850 and 1860 censuses. No matching individual returned. Index coverage gaps or transcription errors cannot be ruled out.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly recorded Patrick Flynn's absence from the 1870 census as negative evidence. The assertion includes appropriate informant attribution (researcher/analyst inference), correctly identifies the evidence_type as 'negative', sets record_role to 'absent', and provides comprehensive bias notes enumerating alternative explanations. The source was properly created and linked. No fabricated material.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill addressed all requirements: logged the nil search (log_006), created a new 1870 census source (src_010/S9) in both research.json and tree.gedcomx.json, and appended the negative assertion (a_014) with full context. The user's request to 'record this as a negative-evidence assertion' was fulfilled in its entirety.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All four tool calls used correct arguments and matched expected behavior. The log_append call captured the nil search properly. The tree_edit call correctly added the source (receiving S9). The research_append calls correctly referenced src_010 and S9 after ensuring S9 existed first. The recovery workflow (second research_append after tree creation) demonstrates appropriate error handling and correct tool usage.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: The assertion is atomic: a_014 contains one fact — Patrick Flynn's absence from the 1870 census. The date (1870), place (Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania), and expected age are properly integrated into a single absence-fact assertion without decomposing unrelated details across multiple fact_types. Census absence is a unified negative finding, correctly expressed as one assertion.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informant is correctly identified as 'Researcher (analyst inference from nil search of 1870 census index)' with informant_proximity set to 'unknown'. The skill properly distinguishes this from a positive census assertion — no household member reported the absence; the researcher inferred it from the search result. The bias_notes clarify that this is an analyst conclusion, not a primary source report, satisfying the requirement for negative evidence informant handling.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: evidence_type is correctly set to 'negative' for a documented absence. The skill treats the nil search result as a direct finding of absence (Patrick was searched for and not found in the index), not indirect inference. record_role is properly set to 'absent' with fact_type 'census', accurately reflecting the source material and the nature of the negative evidence.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly extracted 9 assertions from the census record and properly identified the match to LZNY-BRF. However, the workflow required a workaround: the initial research_append failed because assertions used record_persona_id with a non-sidecar log entry. The skill recovered by calling tree_edit first, then re-submitting with record_persona_id set to null. While the final state is correct, the initial attempt violated schema constraints and required correction. The informant identifications and evidence types are properly assigned.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill addressed all required tasks: extracted 9 atomic assertions covering Patrick Flynn's name, age, birth year, birthplace, residence, and parent-child relationship, plus Thomas Flynn's name, residence, and reciprocal relationship. Added the source to tree.gedcomx.json and research.json. Performed the record_person_matches check as instructed. Provided clear summary of findings supporting the project objective (identifying Patrick Flynn's father as Thomas Flynn).

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill called the correct tools with generally appropriate arguments. record_person_matches was called correctly with id 'MXHY-TP4'. However, the first research_append call included record_persona_id fields when the log entry had no sidecar (results_ref was null), causing validation errors. The skill recognized this, corrected it with tree_edit, then re-submitted with record_persona_id: null. This shows understanding but the initial call violated schema expectations. The second research_append succeeded with corrected arguments.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All nine assertions are properly atomic. Name, age, birth (with date reasoning), birthplace, residence, and relationships are each separate assertions with individual fact_types. The relationship assertions are cleanly separated from their inference methods (noted in informant_bias_notes rather than cramming into the value). Patrick Flynn has distinct assertions for child relationships, Thomas Flynn for parent relationships.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Informant identification is thorough and well-reasoned. For Patrick Flynn's name, age, and birthplace: 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with household_member proximity and clear reasoning (e.g., 'Child age 5 could not self-report; a parent or other household member answered the enumerator'). For residence: 'census enumerator' with witness proximity. For Thomas Flynn's name: 'unknown household member (likely self or spouse)' with household_member proximity. All informant_bias_notes explain the reasoning for the identification.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned throughout. Direct evidence for name, age, birthplace, residence statements that appear explicitly in the record. Indirect evidence for the birth year (computed from age and census year, not directly stated) and for both relationship assertions (1850 census has no relationship column; ParentChild link inferred from household position and indexer inference, as correctly noted in informant_bias_notes). The skill properly recognizes the 1850 census limitation and marks relationships as indirect.

output.text_response:
Now I'll batch the source + 9 assertions in one call:Two fixes needed: add `S1` to tree first, and drop `record_persona_id` (no sidecar). Running `tree_edit` first:S1 assigned. Now the batched `research_append` with `record_persona_id: null` on all assertions:All 10 ops written (src_001, a_001–a_009). Now checking whether this record is already attached to a tree person:All done. Here's the summary:

**Source:** `src_001` / `S1` — "United States Census, 1850," FamilySearch; classification: **original**.

**Assertions (9 total):**
- `child_1` (Patrick Flynn / P1): a_001 name, a_002 age ("age 5", direct), a_003 birth (~1845, indirect — computed from age), a_004 birthplace (Ireland, direct), a_005 residence (Branch Twp, Schuylkill, PA, 1850), a_006 relationship (child_inferred of head, indirect — pre-1880, no relationship column)
- `head_of_household` (Thomas Flynn / P2): a_007 name, a_008 residence (Branch Twp, Schuylkill, PA, 1850), a_009 relationship (parent_inferred of child_1, indirect)

**Tree:** S1 added; no sibling stubs (Thomas Flynn is not yet in `tree.gedcomx.json`, so the trigger condition fails — no in-tree parent to anchor edges; no siblings listed in the record either).

**Match check:** Record MXHY-TP4 is already **accepted** to FamilySearch tree person **LZNY-BRF** (confidence 5, score 0.97). Note that LZNY-BRF is a FamilySearch collaborative-tree PID — person-evidence will need to evaluate whether that maps to our project's I1 (Patrick Flynn).

**Key finding:** Thomas Flynn is the inferred father of the subject; Patrick born ~1845 in Ireland, residing Branch Township, Schuylkill Co., PA in 1850 — directly supports the project objective.

**Next:** Run `check-warnings` on I1, then `person-evidence` to formally link `child_1` → I1 and create/link a stub for Thomas Flynn.

args.notes: Found Patrick Flynn (MXHY-TP4) in household of Thomas Flynn, Branch Township, Schuylkill, Pennsylvania. Record passed directly from search-records.

dimensions.name: Completeness

Added the source to tree.gedcomx.json and research.json. Performed the record_person_matches check as instructed. For residence: 'census enumerator' with witness proximity. For Thomas Flynn's name: 'unknown household member (likely self or spouse)' with household_member proximity. output.text_response:
Good. Let me read the project files and fetch the record in parallel.Now I have what I need. Let me analyze the record:
- **Record:** 1850 U.S. Census, Branch Township, Schuylkill, Pennsylvania
- **Persons:** Patrick Flynn (child, born 1845 Ireland) + Thomas Flynn (head/parent)
- **Relationship:** ParentChild — Thomas → Patrick (indexer-inferred; 1850 census has no relationship column)

`research.json`: no existing sources (next → `src_001`), no questions, `tree.gedcomx.json`: one person `I1` (Patrick Flynn), no sources (next → `S1`). No other children in the record → no sibling stubs needed.

**Step 4 — Log entry first:****Step 5a/5b — Batch source + all assertions:**The schema requires top-level `section`/`op` even with `ops`. Let me provide them alongside the batch:Need to create the `S1` entry in `tree.gedcomx.json` before the source can reference it. Writing tree source first:`S1` created. Now the full batch:All persisted. Here's the terse summary:

---

**Source:** `src_001` → `S1` — *United States Census, 1850*, FamilySearch (derivative), accessed 2026-07-08

**Assertions written: 8 total**
- `child_1` (Patrick Flynn) — `a_001`–`a_005`: name, birth year (~1845, indirect — census recorded age), birthplace (Ireland, direct), residence (Branch Twp, Schuylkill, PA, 1850), relationship to head (indirect — no 1850 relationship column)
- `head_of_household` (Thomas Flynn) — `a_006`–`a_008`: name, residence (Branch Twp, Schuylkill, PA, 1850), relationship as parent of child_1 (indirect)

**Tree changes:** `S1` source description added. No sibling stubs (record has only two persons; no other children present).

**Key finding:** Thomas Flynn named as probable father of Patrick Flynn via 1850 household position — this is **indirect evidence only** (no 1850 relationship column). Birthplace "Ireland" contradicts the project objective's "born in Pennsylvania" — worth flagging.

**Next steps:** Run `check-warnings` on I1, then `person-evidence` to link `child_1` assertions to I1 and evaluate Thomas Flynn as a new parent candidate.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly extracted all facts from the record_read response. All 8 assertions are factually accurate: Patrick Flynn's name, birth year (1845), birthplace (Ireland), and 1850 residence in Branch Township, Schuylkill, Pennsylvania match the record exactly. The ParentChild relationship and Thomas Flynn's facts are correctly extracted and marked as indirect evidence. No fabricated material present.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill comprehensively addressed the extraction task. It extracted all required facts about Patrick Flynn (name, birth date, birthplace, residence), the ParentChild relationship to Thomas Flynn, and Thomas Flynn's details (name, residence). Created source entry linking to the FamilySearch 1850 census collection. All assertions written successfully to research.json and tree source added to tree.gedcomx.json.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All MCP tool calls used correct arguments. record_read was called with recordId 'ark:/61903/1:1:68Q9-K34P' matching expected_args (~68Q9-K34P). research_log_append and tree_edit calls were live operations with no expected_args specified. research_append calls correctly structured the source and assertion entries using proper schema.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: All 8 assertions are properly atomic. Each assertion contains a single fact: separate entries for name, birth year, birthplace, residence, and relationship rather than compound values. For example, birth year and birthplace are separate assertions (a_002 and a_003), and residence is its own assertion (a_004). The relationship assertion (a_005) focuses on the single inferred parent-child relationship.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informants are correctly identified with appropriate proximity. For Patrick Flynn's name, birthplace, and birth year: 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' and detailed bias notes explaining the child's age and who would report. For residence and relationship: 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness'. Informant identification distinguishes reporters from recorders appropriately throughout.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned. Birthplace (Ireland) is marked direct—stated in the record. Birth year (1845) is indirect—computed by FamilySearch indexer from age. Name is direct—explicitly stated. Residence is direct—observable fact recorded by enumerator. ParentChild relationship (a_005) is correctly marked indirect with explanation that 1850 census has no relationship column and this inference comes from household position only.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All assertions extracted from the 1850 census record are factually accurate and properly sourced. The skill correctly identified Patrick Flynn's name, age 5, birth year ~1845, birthplace Ireland, and 1850 residence in Branch Township, Schuylkill, Pennsylvania. The 1860 census match (MABC, confidence 4, score 0.88) and Pennsylvania Death Certificates match (MDEF, confidence 3, score 0.65) were accurately reported from the FamilySearch record_record_matches API call. No fabricated claims or contradictions with the source data.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill fully addressed all requirements: extracted all 8 assertions from the 1850 census record (name, age, birth, birthplace, residence, gender, relationship, and Thomas Flynn's name); created a source entry with proper citation; logged the search in research.json; successfully called record_record_matches with the correct ID (MXHY-TP4); and reported both pending collateral matches (1860 census and death certificates). The skill appropriately highlighted the 1860 census as higher-priority due to confidence 4 score.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All MCP tool calls passed arguments matching the fixture expectations. The record_record_matches call correctly used id='MXHY-TP4' as expected. The research_log_append call correctly specified 'user_provided' as the tool source since the record was handed over by search-records. The tree_edit and research_append calls properly managed the source S1 creation before appending assertions, correcting the initial sequencing issue. All identifier fields and query parameters match the test expectations.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Every assertion represents a single extractable fact. Patrick Flynn's name, age, birth year, birthplace, residence, and gender are each separate assertions with distinct fact_types. The relationship assertion (child of head of household) is isolated from name/age/birthplace. Thomas Flynn's name is a separate assertion. No compound claims; each value field contains only one fact. Downstream skills can query individual facts independently.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Informant identification
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly identified specific informants with proximity reasoning. For name/age/birthplace/gender of child Patrick and name of head Thomas: 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' and detailed bias_notes explaining the reasoning (e.g., age 5 child would be reported by parent). For residence: 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness' and explanation that the enumerator observed the household location directly. Informant distinctions properly separate the actual reporter from the recorder.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types correctly reflect the source's content. Name, age, birthplace, gender, and residence are direct evidence (stated explicitly in the census record or columns). Birth year (~1845) is correctly marked indirect because it was computed from age 5 in 1850, not directly stated. The relationship (child of head) is correctly indirect because the 1850 census lacks an explicit relationship column and the relationship is inferred from household position. All classifications align with the source's actual structure and the skill's interpretation of evidence standards.

Both files are read. The project is fresh — no sources, log entries, or assertions yet. The only tree person is I1 (Patrick Flynn). No household parent exists in the tree, so the 5d sibling-stub trigger will not fire.

**Plan:**
- Role: Patrick = `child_1` (born 1845, age ~5 in 1850), Thomas Flynn = `head_of_household` (name only from record title; not in stub GedcomX)
- No staged sidecar/resultsRef → log as `user_provided`, `record_persona_id: null` on all assertions
- Source: `src_001` / `S1` (both project-first)
- Assertions: 8 total (6 for child_1 Patrick, 1 relationship indirect, 1 name for Thomas as head)

Step 4 — log entry first, then batch-write:`log_001` in hand. Now batch-writing the source + 8 assertions in one call:Need to write `S1` to the tree first, then the `research_append` batch can reference it. Fixing order:`S1` written. Now the full source + assertions batch:All persisted. Now checking FamilySearch record matches for persona MXHY-TP4:---

**Extraction complete — summary:**

- **Source:** `src_001` / `S1` — *United States Census, 1850*, FamilySearch; classified **original**
- **Assertions written:** 8 total
  - `child_1` (Patrick Flynn, `a_001–a_007`): Name · Age (5, direct) · Birth year (~1845, indirect) · Birthplace (Ireland, direct) · Residence (Branch Twp, Schuylkill, PA, 1850) · Gender · Relationship (position consistent with child of head — **indirect**, no relationship column in 1850 census)
  - `head_of_household` (Thomas Flynn, `a_008`): Name only — full stub GedcomX did not include Thomas's persona; a **live `record_read` of this ARK is recommended** to capture his age, birthplace, and all other household members
- **Tree changes:** `S1` added; no sibling stubs (Thomas Flynn not yet in tree, trigger condition unmet)

**FamilySearch record matches for MXHY-TP4 (informational only):**

| Record | Confidence | Score | Status |
|--------|-----------|-------|--------|
| 1860 U.S. Federal Census (MABC) | **4** | 0.88 | pending |
| Pennsylvania Death Certificates, 1906–1967 (MDEF) | 3 | 0.65 | pending |

The **1860 census match (confidence 4)** is high-priority — it would place Patrick at age ~15 and may name the same household, corroborating the Thomas Flynn relationship. The death certificate match aligns with the known 1908 death in Schuylkill County.

**Next steps:**
1. `check-warnings` on I1
2. `person-evidence` to link `a_001–a_007` to I1
3. Live `record_read` of this ARK to extract Thomas Flynn's full facts (age, birthplace → critical for the research objective)
4. `search-records` to pull the 1860 census match (MABC) into a new extraction pass

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All assertions extracted from the 1850 census record are factually accurate and properly sourced. The skill correctly identified Patrick Flynn's name, age 5, birth year ~1845, birthplace Ireland, and 1850 residence in Branch Township, Schuylkill, Pennsylvania. The 1860 census match (MABC, confidence 4, score 0.88) and Pennsylvania Death Certificates match (MDEF, confidence 3, score 0.65) were accurately reported from the FamilySearch record_record_matches API call. No fabricated claims or contradictions with the source data.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill fully addressed all requirements: extracted all 8 assertions from the 1850 census record (name, age, birth, birthplace, residence, gender, relationship, and Thomas Flynn's name); created a source entry with proper citation; logged the search in research.json; successfully called record_record_matches with the correct ID (MXHY-TP4); and reported both pending collateral matches (1860 census and death certificates). The skill appropriately highlighted the 1860 census as higher-priority due to confidence 4 score.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All MCP tool calls passed arguments matching the fixture expectations. The record_record_matches call correctly used id='MXHY-TP4' as expected. The research_log_append call correctly specified 'user_provided' as the tool source since the record was handed over by search-records. The tree_edit and research_append calls properly managed the source S1 creation before appending assertions, correcting the initial sequencing issue. All identifier fields and query parameters match the test expectations.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Every assertion represents a single extractable fact. Patrick Flynn's name, age, birth year, birthplace, residence, and gender are each separate assertions with distinct fact_types. The relationship assertion (child of head of household) is isolated from name/age/birthplace. Thomas Flynn's name is a separate assertion. No compound claims; each value field contains only one fact. Downstream skills can query individual facts independently.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly identified specific informants with proximity reasoning. For name/age/birthplace/gender of child Patrick and name of head Thomas: 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' and detailed bias_notes explaining the reasoning (e.g., age 5 child would be reported by parent). For residence: 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness' and explanation that the enumerator observed the household location directly. Informant distinctions properly separate the actual reporter from the recorder.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types correctly reflect the source's content. Name, age, birthplace, gender, and residence are direct evidence (stated explicitly in the census record or columns). Birth year (~1845) is correctly marked indirect because it was computed from age 5 in 1850, not directly stated. The relationship (child of head) is correctly indirect because the 1850 census lacks an explicit relationship column and the relationship is inferred from household position. All classifications align with the source's actual structure and the skill's interpretation of evidence standards.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Correctness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All 13 assertions are factually correct and properly supported by the census record data. Names, age (5), birth year (~1845 derived from age), birthplace (Ireland), and residence (Branch Township, Schuylkill, Pennsylvania) match the gedcomx input. All three persons (P1, P2, P3) are correctly extracted with appropriate record_role assignments (child_1, head_of_household, wife). Relationship assertions are correctly marked as indirect evidence (1850 census had no explicit relationship column). Record IDs use full ARK URL verbatim. Log entry reference (log_001) correctly points to pre-existing search-records entry. No fabricated material.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Completeness
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: All extractable assertions from the gedcomx were captured: 7 for Patrick (name, age, birth year, birthplace, residence, two parent relationships), 3 for Thomas (name, residence, father relationship), and 3 for Mary (name, residence, mother relationship). The source metadata was properly created and linked via log_001. The skill correctly skipped sibling stub generation after checking that neither parent exists in the tree. All facts from the record were systematically converted to assertions.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Tool Arguments

While the skill self-corrected by calling tree_edit (which succeeded and assigned S1), then re-running research_append successfully, the initial sequencing error was a meaningful mismatch. The retry pattern and all subsequent tool arguments were correct (full ARK URLs, proper persona IDs, accurate field values), but the ordering mistake represents a non-critical but avoidable inefficiency.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Every assertion decomposes facts into atomic, single-fact entries. Name, age, birth year, and birthplace are separate assertions. Residence is isolated. Two relationship assertions (child of Thomas, child of Mary) are separate. aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Informants are specifically identified with justified reasoning. For Patrick's name/age/birthplace: 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' and rationale explaining a 5-year-old could not self-report. For residence: 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness'—correctly distinguishing recorder from reporter. For adult Thomas/Mary names: 'unknown household member (likely self or spouse)' with reasoning that adults typically self-report. All informant_bias_notes explain why that person would know the fact, addressing proximity to the event.

aggregated_dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
aggregated_dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned per the 1850 census characteristics. Name, age, and birthplace for Patrick are marked 'direct' (stated in the record), while birth year is 'indirect' (computed from age + census year—not explicitly listed). Relationship assertions (parent-child) are correctly marked 'indirect' because the 1850 census did not include explicit relationship columns; relationships were inferred from household position by the FamilySearch indexer. Residence facts are 'direct' (recorded by enumerator as witness). Classification enables proper downstream conflict resolution—indirect vs. direct is correctly distinguished throughout.

Now I have everything I need. Let me plan the extraction:

**Roles:** P1 Patrick = `child_1`, P2 Thomas = `head_of_household`, P3 Mary = `wife`  
**Source:** `src_001` (first source, no existing ones)  
**Log:** `log_001` already written by search-records — no new log entry needed  

**Sibling/stub check:**
- Subject (Patrick) is `child_1` ✓  
- Parents in tree: Thomas Flynn → not in tree; Mary Flynn → not in tree  
- Trigger NOT MET (no household parent exists in `tree.gedcomx.json`) → skip sibling stubs  

**Assertions planned (13):**  
- P1: name, age, birth year (indirect), birth_place, residence, relationship×2 (to Thomas, to Mary)  
- P2: name, residence, relationship (parent of Patrick)  
- P3: name, residence, relationship (parent of Patrick)  The source description must exist in the tree before I can reference it. Adding S1 first, then the full batch.All writes confirmed. Here's the summary:

**Source:** `src_001` / `S1` — U.S. Census, 1850 (original, FamilySearch, log_001)

**Assertions: 13 total**
- `child_1` (Patrick Flynn / P1) — `a_001`–`a_007`: name, age (5), birth year ~1845 (indirect), birthplace Ireland, residence Branch Township Schuylkill PA, relationship×2 (child of Thomas, child of Mary)
- `head_of_household` (Thomas Flynn / P2) — `a_008`–`a_010`: name, residence, relationship (father of Patrick, inferred)
- `wife` (Mary Flynn / P3) — `a_011`–`a_013`: name, residence, relationship (mother of Patrick, inferred)

**Tree:** `S1` added; no sibling stubs (trigger not met — neither Thomas nor Mary exists in `tree.gedcomx.json` yet)

**Key findings:**
- Thomas Flynn and Mary Flynn are both inferred parents of Patrick — directly relevant to the project objective
- All parent–child relationships are `indirect` (no relationship column in the 1850 census)
- **Potential conflict:** project objective states Patrick was "born ca. 1845 in Pennsylvania," but this record gives birthplace as **Ireland** — worth flagging for conflict-resolution after person-evidence links are made
- Birthplace is national-level only (Ireland); county/parish unknown

**Next step:** run `person-evidence` to link P1→I1 and P2/P3 to new parent stubs, then `check-warnings` for plausibility checks.

The judge will grade other dimensions but won't score tool work explicitly. Consider adding a tool-usage dimension or renaming an existing one.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: All 13 assertions are factually correct and properly supported by the census record data. Names, age (5), birth year (~1845 derived from age), birthplace (Ireland), and residence (Branch Township, Schuylkill, Pennsylvania) match the gedcomx input. All three persons (P1, P2, P3) are correctly extracted with appropriate record_role assignments (child_1, head_of_household, wife). Relationship assertions are correctly marked as indirect evidence (1850 census had no explicit relationship column). Record IDs use full ARK URL verbatim. Log entry reference (log_001) correctly points to pre-existing search-records entry. No fabricated material.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: All extractable assertions from the gedcomx were captured: 7 for Patrick (name, age, birth year, birthplace, residence, two parent relationships), 3 for Thomas (name, residence, father relationship), and 3 for Mary (name, residence, mother relationship). The source metadata was properly created and linked via log_001. The skill correctly skipped sibling stub generation after checking that neither parent exists in the tree. All facts from the record were systematically converted to assertions.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: First research_append call failed with 'gedcomx_source_description_id S1 not found in tree.gedcomx.json sources' — the skill attempted to reference S1 before tree_edit had created it. While the skill self-corrected by calling tree_edit (which succeeded and assigned S1), then re-running research_append successfully, the initial sequencing error was a meaningful mismatch. The retry pattern and all subsequent tool arguments were correct (full ARK URLs, proper persona IDs, accurate field values), but the ordering mistake represents a non-critical but avoidable inefficiency.

dimensions.name: Assertion atomicity
dimensions.rationale: Every assertion decomposes facts into atomic, single-fact entries. Name, age, birth year, and birthplace are separate assertions. Residence is isolated. Two relationship assertions (child of Thomas, child of Mary) are separate. The compound fact 'age + birth year' is properly split: age is direct (stated in 1850), birth year is indirect (computed from age). Each assertion carries one fact_type and one value—no compound claims crammed together. Downstream person-evidence linking can query individual facts.

dimensions.name: Informant identification
dimensions.rationale: Informants are specifically identified with justified reasoning. For Patrick's name/age/birthplace: 'unknown household member (likely a parent)' with proximity 'household_member' and rationale explaining a 5-year-old could not self-report. For residence: 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness'—correctly distinguishing recorder from reporter. For adult Thomas/Mary names: 'unknown household member (likely self or spouse)' with reasoning that adults typically self-report. All informant_bias_notes explain why that person would know the fact, addressing proximity to the event.

dimensions.name: Evidence type accuracy
dimensions.rationale: Evidence types are correctly assigned per the 1850 census characteristics. Name, age, and birthplace for Patrick are marked 'direct' (stated in the record), while birth year is 'indirect' (computed from age + census year—not explicitly listed). Relationship assertions (parent-child) are correctly marked 'indirect' because the 1850 census did not include explicit relationship columns; relationships were inferred from household position by the FamilySearch indexer. Residence facts are 'direct' (recorded by enumerator as witness). Classification enables proper downstream conflict resolution—indirect vs. direct is correctly distinguished throughout.
